name: Our Revolution
subject: NEWS: Federal strategy, Bernie on a roll, St. Louis power slate, new OR groups, and more!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

INSIDER NEWS FROM THE FRONTLINES OF THE POLITICAL REVOLUTION

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

HEADLINES: Jayapal on Executive Actions | Bernie on a Roll! | St. Louis
Power Slate | Meet Alisha Sonnier! | Next Inside the Party | New Group! SF
Education Alliance | New Labor Secretary? | & MORE!

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1011&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

"Are you willing to fight for that person who you don't even know as much
as you're willing to fight for yourself?” — Bernie Sanders

The ruling class counts dividing us to stop change. But if we’re willing
to love and fight for one another, their tactics are no match for our
shared solidarity.

Get this new sticker with a donation of $10 or more — and just $5 for
members! With our collective contributions, we are taking on corruption
and corporate power to build a better world!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8289?t=1012&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]GET YOUR STICKER

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1013&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1014&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

On our [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8367?t=1015&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]National Organize-to-Win Call Monday night, US Rep. Pramila
Jayapal laid out our [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8368?t=1016&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]theory of change and how Biden can use executive
action to deliver under a divided Congress.

Chair of the Congressional Progressive Caucus, Jayapal said the caucus
will be organizing with Our Revolution members in an inside-outside
strategy.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1017&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

Rep. Jaypal [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8369?t=1018&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]set out a path under a divided Congress, including pushing
President Biden to use the power of the pen on housing, higher wages for
care workers, and more. He can also crack down on Big Pharma, the energy
sector, and employers withholding overtime pay, she said.

And, Jayapal believes we may be able to get bipartisan legislation on
issues like antitrust — key in the battle against corporate power.

Finally, she said it’s time to prepare for the next big takeover of
Congress in 2024, when we can grow our power at every level of government.

What races are you eyeing in 2024? We’ve already started a fund to
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8291?t=1019&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]defeat Sen. Kyrsten Sinema!

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1020&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

Our Revolution members identified 5 top priorities for Biden to use the
power of the pen: climate action, economic/tax fairness, lower healthcare
costs, worker power/wages, and equal justice under the law.

These will be the focus as our Federal Action Team members organize with
partner groups and elected allies. There’s a path, so let’s get to work!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1021&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

Be part of the action and help our coalition push Congress and the White
House to deliver for all of us.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8220?t=1022&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]JOIN THE FEDERAL ACTION TEAM

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1023&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

CHAIRMAN BERNIE & FEDERAL FIGHTS

Bernie Sanders is on a roll as the new Chairman of the Senate HELP
Committee. He’s taking on [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8370?t=1024&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]Big Pharma, calling out Moderna for raising
prices, and he’s calling Starbucks CEO Howard Schultz to testify on
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8371?t=1025&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]union-busting.

Bernie has a plan to expand Social Security against right wing pushes for
cuts, and he’s introducing legislation for $60k minimum pay for
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8372?t=1026&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]teachers!

On the House side, Rep. Pramila Jayapal is leading an [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8373?t=1027&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]opposition to
Medicare Advantage — an historic step in the fight against Medicare
privatization.

And, Rep. Ro Khanna is working to pass a Windfall Profits Tax on Big Oil —
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/signup/ro_khanna_petition/?source=EM_NBI_OR_20230217_PT_RoKhanna_0_3MO_OurRevolution_NA_NA_RoKhanna&t=8&t=1028&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]SIGN PETITION HERE

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/7070?t=1029&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]Become a monthly donor to support Our Revolution and our
inside-outside work with progressive partners in Congress!

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1030&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

On Valentine’s Day, our coalition rallied to show love to servers and
organize for higher wages in NYC, Baltimore, Chicago and cities across the
country.

We're partnering with One Fair Wage and putting bills on the table to end
the subminimum wage in states around the country. Now, it’s time to
pressure elected officials to pass them, and we’re taking on the
restaurant lobby to do it.

Call your State Reps and tell them to end subminimum wages if you live in
the following states:

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8374?t=1031&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]NEW YORK | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8375?t=1032&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]MARYLAND | [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8376?t=1033&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]ILLINOIS

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1034&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

If you’re anywhere in the country, we can use your help to raise the wage
and end the subminimum wages in states across the country, and we’re
lobbying state lawmakers to pass them.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8222?t=1035&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]JOIN THE TEAM

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1036&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

SENATORS JOIN OUR FIGHT vs. ‘OTHER NRA’

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8377?t=1037&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]Rolling Stone broke the news that Senators Bernie, Warren, Merkley,
and others joined our stand against the ‘Other NRA’ for taking $25M of
workers’ money to keep wages low.

“You owe workers an answer as to why you are secretly using their funds to
lobby against their interests,” the Senators wrote.

Thanks to NRA lobbying, the federal minimum wage has been stuck at
$7.25/hr for 14 years, and the federal tipped wage has remained $2.13/hr
since 1991!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8378?t=1038&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]WATCH BERNIE’S VIDEO ON OUR CAMPAIGN!

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1039&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

Call your Reps and demand they reject dirty money from ‘Other NRA’. US
Reps. Ro Khanna and Greg Casar, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, and others have
already signed on!

When we told new House Speaker Hakeem Jeffries where the money came from,
he returned it, and we’re urging more elected officials to do the same.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8247?t=1040&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]CALL YOUR REP!

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8379?t=1041&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

NEXT LABOR SECRETARY?

Between Sean Patrick Maloney and California Labor Secretary Julie Su for
Secretary of Labor, OR Exec Director Joseph Geevarghese told [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8379?t=1042&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]The Hill:

“Fighting for workers is in her DNA, and she has a track record of doing
it,” Joseph said of Su, adding that “Maloney is no friend of labor” and is
“aligned with the corporate wing of the Democratic Party.”

He also suggested former Rep. Andy Levin for the role. Levin was
reportedly considered before Marty Walsh was named in 2021.

“To me, the most significant data point is that union density is at an
all-time low,” Joseph said. “President Biden talks about support for
workers on strike, but at the end of the day, he as president hasn’t used
his power to add more workers to the ranks.”

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/7070?t=1043&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]These are the decisions that have reverberating impacts for workers
and our families. Join Our Revolution to grow our progressive voice and
stand with labor.

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8380?t=1044&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

★ ★ ★

BUILDING LOCAL POWER IN ST. LOUIS

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1045&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

St. Louis is an exciting place for progressive organizing right now — from
electing US Rep. Cori Bush in 2020 to Mayor Tishuara Jones and a
progressive majority on the Board of Aldermen in 2021.

Since new Board President Megan Green completed the progressive trifecta
in November, the city has passed a Basic Income trial, pushed for tenant’s
rights and much more!

Now, the whole St. Louis Board is up for grabs again in redrawn districts,
and Our Revolution has an amazing slate on the ballot to hold the
progressive majority — from re-electing Megan Green to candidates in 10
ward races.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8364?t=1046&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]PHONEBANK FOR OUR ST. LOUIS SLATE!

★ ★ ★

MEET ALISHA SONNIER

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1047&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

One of those candidates progressive powerhouse Alilsha Sonnier who we are
proud to back for St. Louis Alderwoman in Ward 7!

Alisha's [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8381?t=1048&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]activism began with the murder of Michael Brown, and she's
worked with the Fight for $15, Pro Choice Missouri, as political director
for Megan Green’s campaign, and now on the St. Louis Board of Education.

“This story starts with the death of a black young man left in the street
for 4.5 hours less than a decade ago — to now some of the city’s highest
seats being held by the people aligned with a people-centered agenda,”
Alisha [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8367?t=1049&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]said Monday.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1050&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

Alisha spoke on housing, healthcare, education, and ending cash bail: “The
thing that stands between people and their freedom should not be whether
or not they can afford it.”

“I want to see accountability for the people with the power and the
money,” Alisha said. “I’m not asking for anything extreme, I just want to
see them pay their fair share.”

“When we’re building this political movement, we’ve got to advocate for
our people and our city like it’s do or die because it is,” she said. “We
can show a national model of what progressive leadership can look like.”

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8364?t=1051&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]MAKE CALLS FOR ALISHA

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1052&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1053&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

OR Board Chair and Nebraska Party Chair Jane Kleeb joined our [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8367?t=1054&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]National
Organizing Call to talk progressive party-building — from the grassroots
to the DNC.

“There are changes happening even though we sometimes get setbacks like
with banning dark money,” Jane said. The resolutions committee refused to
allow a vote, [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8382?t=1055&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]Common Dreams reported.

Our progressive bloc is growing, and Jane called on us to plan now for
upcoming delegate elections to get more say over election rules, party
platforms and more.

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1056&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

Progressives now hold leadership positions in 20 state parties, and we
cannot cede this ground to the corporate wing. We’ll be fighting for
progressive leaders in Nevada, Florida, Oregon, and other states soon!

Join our Party Transformation Team to help make a party that is supportive
of progressive ideas and more responsive to the grassroots.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8227?t=1057&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]JOIN THE TEAM

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8387?t=1058&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

NEW GROUP! The San Francisco Education Alliance, powered by Our
Revolution, just launched!

“Pushing back against recalls and privatization, a group of local
progressives affiliated with Our Revolution, has launched SF Education
Alliance...‘proudly left-leaning, anti-racist, anti-fascist,
anti-carceral, pro-union,’” the [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8383?t=1060&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]San Francisco Standard reported.

Organizers Jess Nguyen and Allyson Eddy Bravvman [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8384?t=1061&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]laid out the vision
to protect public schools, advance progressive policies, mobilize the
community, and hold elected officials accountable.

“Our public schools are at an inflection point, but when I think of Our
Revolution, I think of grassroots activism and people power,” said SF
Education Board Commissioner Alida Fisher. “That’s what drives me every
day.”

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8385?t=1062&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]JOIN THE SF EDUCATION ALLIANCE

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8383?t=1063&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ][IMG]

Our Brooklyn chapter, Brooklyn Progressive Action Network, just
re-launched!

There was food and fun, and we organized for the single-payer NY Health
Act, the Good Cause Eviction Bill, exposing dark money PAC donors, and
more.

Brooklyn members were joined by Public Advocate and founding BPAN member
Jumaane Williams, Comptroller Brad Lander, Assemblyman Brian Cunningham,
and other elected officials.

sit down and organize with more great guests and progressive leaders!

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/340?t=1067&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]RSVP

As the country's largest grassroots-funded progressive political
organization, we are carrying out Bernie’s vision through our 4-POINT PLAN
TO WIN:

Transform the Dem Party

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/7070?t=1069&akid=4032%2E5885482%2EXv8Giw ]BECOME A MOVEMENT BUILDER

Medical Debt is a uniquely American problem among the so-called “developed
world" — and a big one that has been largely neglected by politicians and
the media.

We’re compiling stories to bring pressure to bear on this great healthcare
injustice. If you’re one of the many who owe money to a hospital, please

industry and experienced low wages, we encourage you to speak out — we’ll
have your back!

SF Education Alliance | New Labor Secretary? | & MORE! ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] "Are you willing to fight for that person who you don't even know as much as you're willing to fight for yourself?” — Bernie Sanders The ruling class counts dividing us to stop change. But if we’re willing to love and fight for one another, their tactics are no match for our shared solidarity. Get this new sticker with a donation of $10 or more — and just $5 for members! With our collective contributions, we are taking on corruption and corporate power to build a better world! [ ]GET YOUR STICKER ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] On our [ ]National Organize-to-Win Call Monday night, US Rep. Pramila Jayapal laid out our [ ]theory of change and how Biden can use executive action to deliver under a divided Congress. Chair of the Congressional Progressive Caucus, Jayapal said the caucus will be organizing with Our Revolution members in an inside-outside strategy. [ ][IMG] Rep. Jaypal [ ]set out a path under a divided Congress, including pushing President Biden to use the power of the pen on housing, higher wages for care workers, and more. He can also crack down on Big Pharma, the energy sector, and employers withholding overtime pay, she said. And, Jayapal believes we may be able to get bipartisan legislation on issues like antitrust — key in the battle against corporate power. Finally, she said it’s time to prepare for the next big takeover of Congress in 2024, when we can grow our power at every level of government. What races are you eyeing in 2024? We’ve already started a fund to [ ]defeat Sen. Kyrsten Sinema! ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] Our Revolution members identified 5 top priorities for Biden to use the power of the pen: climate action, economic/tax fairness, lower healthcare costs, worker power/wages, and equal justice under the law. These will be the focus as our Federal Action Team members organize with partner groups and elected allies. There’s a path, so let’s get to work! [ ][IMG] Be part of the action and help our coalition push Congress and the White House to deliver for all of us. [ ]JOIN THE FEDERAL ACTION TEAM ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] CHAIRMAN BERNIE & FEDERAL FIGHTS Bernie Sanders is on a roll as the new Chairman of the Senate HELP Committee. He’s taking on [ ]Big Pharma, calling out Moderna for raising prices, and he’s calling Starbucks CEO Howard Schultz to testify on [ ]union-busting. Bernie has a plan to expand Social Security against right wing pushes for cuts, and he’s introducing legislation for $60k minimum pay for [ ]teachers! On the House side, Rep. Pramila Jayapal is leading an [ ]opposition to Medicare Advantage — an historic step in the fight against Medicare privatization. And, Rep. Ro Khanna is working to pass a Windfall Profits Tax on Big Oil — [ ]SIGN PETITION HERE [ ]Become a monthly donor to support Our Revolution and our inside-outside work with progressive partners in Congress! ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] On Valentine’s Day, our coalition rallied to show love to servers and organize for higher wages in NYC, Baltimore, Chicago and cities across the country. We're partnering with One Fair Wage and putting bills on the table to end the subminimum wage in states around the country. Now, it’s time to pressure elected officials to pass them, and we’re taking on the restaurant lobby to do it. Call your State Reps and tell them to end subminimum wages if you live in the following states: [ ]NEW YORK | [ ]MARYLAND | [ ]ILLINOIS ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] If you’re anywhere in the country, we can use your help to raise the wage and end the subminimum wages in states across the country, and we’re lobbying state lawmakers to pass them. [ ]JOIN THE TEAM ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] SENATORS JOIN OUR FIGHT vs. ‘OTHER NRA’ [ ]Rolling Stone broke the news that Senators Bernie, Warren, Merkley, and others joined our stand against the ‘Other NRA’ for taking $25M of workers’ money to keep wages low. “You owe workers an answer as to why you are secretly using their funds to lobby against their interests,” the Senators wrote. Thanks to NRA lobbying, the federal minimum wage has been stuck at $7.25/hr for 14 years, and the federal tipped wage has remained $2.13/hr since 1991! [ ]WATCH BERNIE’S VIDEO ON OUR CAMPAIGN! ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] Call your Reps and demand they reject dirty money from ‘Other NRA’. US Reps. Ro Khanna and Greg Casar, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, and others have already signed on! When we told new House Speaker Hakeem Jeffries where the money came from, he returned it, and we’re urging more elected officials to do the same. [ ]CALL YOUR REP! ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] NEXT LABOR SECRETARY? Between Sean Patrick Maloney and California Labor Secretary Julie Su for Secretary of Labor, OR Exec Director Joseph Geevarghese told [ ]The Hill: “Fighting for workers is in her DNA, and she has a track record of doing it,” Joseph said of Su, adding that “Maloney is no friend of labor” and is “aligned with the corporate wing of the Democratic Party.” He also suggested former Rep. Andy Levin for the role. Levin was reportedly considered before Marty Walsh was named in 2021. “To me, the most significant data point is that union density is at an all-time low,” Joseph said. “President Biden talks about support for workers on strike, but at the end of the day, he as president hasn’t used his power to add more workers to the ranks.” [ ]These are the decisions that have reverberating impacts for workers and our families. Join Our Revolution to grow our progressive voice and stand with labor. ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] ★ ★ ★ BUILDING LOCAL POWER IN ST. LOUIS [ ][IMG] St. Louis is an exciting place for progressive organizing right now — from electing US Rep. Cori Bush in 2020 to Mayor Tishuara Jones and a progressive majority on the Board of Aldermen in 2021. Since new Board President Megan Green completed the progressive trifecta in November, the city has passed a Basic Income trial, pushed for tenant’s rights and much more! Now, the whole St. Louis Board is up for grabs again in redrawn districts, and Our Revolution has an amazing slate on the ballot to hold the progressive majority — from re-electing Megan Green to candidates in 10 ward races. [ ]PHONEBANK FOR OUR ST. LOUIS SLATE! ★ ★ ★ MEET ALISHA SONNIER [ ][IMG] One of those candidates progressive powerhouse Alilsha Sonnier who we are proud to back for St. Louis Alderwoman in Ward 7! Alisha's [ ]activism began with the murder of Michael Brown, and she's worked with the Fight for $15, Pro Choice Missouri, as political director for Megan Green’s campaign, and now on the St. Louis Board of Education. “This story starts with the death of a black young man left in the street for 4.5 hours less than a decade ago — to now some of the city’s highest seats being held by the people aligned with a people-centered agenda,” Alisha [ ]said Monday. [ ][IMG] Alisha spoke on housing, healthcare, education, and ending cash bail: “The thing that stands between people and their freedom should not be whether or not they can afford it.” “I want to see accountability for the people with the power and the money,” Alisha said. “I’m not asking for anything extreme, I just want to see them pay their fair share.” “When we’re building this political movement, we’ve got to advocate for our people and our city like it’s do or die because it is,” she said. “We can show a national model of what progressive leadership can look like.” [ ]MAKE CALLS FOR ALISHA ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] OR Board Chair and Nebraska Party Chair Jane Kleeb joined our [ ]National Organizing Call to talk progressive party-building — from the grassroots to the DNC. “There are changes happening even though we sometimes get setbacks like with banning dark money,” Jane said. The resolutions committee refused to allow a vote, [ ]Common Dreams reported. Our progressive bloc is growing, and Jane called on us to plan now for upcoming delegate elections to get more say over election rules, party platforms and more. ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] Progressives now hold leadership positions in 20 state parties, and we cannot cede this ground to the corporate wing. We’ll be fighting for progressive leaders in Nevada, Florida, Oregon, and other states soon! Join our Party Transformation Team to help make a party that is supportive of progressive ideas and more responsive to the grassroots. [ ]JOIN THE TEAM ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] NEW GROUP! The San Francisco Education Alliance, powered by Our Revolution, just launched! “Pushing back against recalls and privatization, a group of local progressives affiliated with Our Revolution, has launched SF Education Alliance...‘proudly left-leaning, anti-racist, anti-fascist, anti-carceral, pro-union,’” the [ ]San Francisco Standard reported. [Would you please donate of any amount right away?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58917934/392651303/-1322984316?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_JP_20230218_FR_MMFR_0_3MO_TeamPanetta_NA_NA_MMFR1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ODhmMWEyMC1jNGFmLWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lTZeZ6Gsz54TBbNKQcVslix660nvV95P4ePvoBPk9V4=)

**_– – – Original Message from Jimmy Panetta – – –_**

Peter,

We face many challenges in California’s new 19th Congressional District, including the recovery efforts for the recent storms, high cost of living, and lack of affordable housing. Please know that I hear your concerns on these issues.

Because of what I know about the people of the new 19th District, I am more hopeful than ever that our shared senses of community and values will help get us where we need to be.

In order to continue the important work of representing your needs in Congress, I need your help. [My team and I need to close a $6,500 fundraising gap by this weekend to keep us on track with this month’s fundraising goal. May I count on you to show your support by chipping in a donation?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/58917935/392651304/-1322984316?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_JP_20230218_FR_MMFR_0_3MO_TeamPanetta_NA_NA_MMFR1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ODhmMWEyMC1jNGFmLWVkMTEtOTk0ZC0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lTZeZ6Gsz54TBbNKQcVslix660nvV95P4ePvoBPk9V4=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately:_ [DONATE $10 clean_body: | Peter, With our mid-month fundraising deadline just days away, we wanted to triple-check you got Jimmy’s message. = – – – Original Message from Jimmy Panetta – – – Peter, We face many challenges in California’s new 19th Congressional District, including the recovery efforts for the recent storms, high cost of living, and lack of affordable housing. Please know that I hear your concerns on these issues. Because of what I know about the people of the new 19th District, I am more hopeful than ever that our shared senses of community and values will help get us where we need to be. In order to continue the important work of representing your needs in Congress, I need your help. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately: = = = = = = The sooner I hit this goal, the sooner I can stop fundraising this month and get back to focusing entirely on what matters most — fighting for our community. Thank you, Jimmy If you'd like to receive only the most important updates from us, Authorized and Paid for by Jimmy Panetta for Congress 2024, FEC ID# C00592154 Jimmy Panetta for Congress PO Box 103 Carmel Valley, CA 93924 United States ** **

**We’ve seen the difference grassroots support makes in these races – it powered our candidates to incredible victories in Pennsylvania and Virginia earlier this year. Our opponents in these races are right-wing Republicans who will help push forward the GOP’s hateful platform of dangerous abortion bans, attacks on the LGBTQ+ community, and set up barriers to the ballot box ahead of the next presidential election.
**These special elections represent critical opportunities to build our Democratic power and elect more Democrats who will fight back against GOP extremism in their state legislatures.**
Special elections are constantly overlooked and underfunded – but these races are a key part of our strategy to build Democratic majorities in statehouses across the country. By investing in these races today, you’ll own a piece of every Democratic victory in 2023, 2024, and beyond.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Catherine=20Engelbrecht=20|=20True=20the=20Vote?=
subject: Recusal, Rebuttal, and Other Truths from the Front Line
body:
Events of the week and a look back at how we got here | | | | | |
---
| | **Recusal, Rebuttal, and Other Truths from the Front Line.**
Lots of new news here at True the Vote. Before we dive in, I want to say thank you to all of you who stand with us, support us, and help spread the word.
_“The world offers you comfort. But you were not made for comfort. You were made for greatness”_ _― Pope Benedict XVI_
We are on full court defense in five critical lawsuits, researching, writing, making videos, event planning, developing new tech and training programs, and working side by side with sheriffs across the country - all part of a national plan for fair and honest elections. Every day is a fight. But we are here for such a time.
Never quit.
Ever onward -
---
[**PS If you can, please donate to support our work. We need you**](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=e8511472d3&e=ad8cba7e98)**r help.**
|
---
**Recusal | Federal Judge Recuses in Konnech v. True the Vote**
True the Vote issued this statement on the recusal of Judge Kenneth Hoyt from ongoing litigation with Konnech, an international election software provider, over alleged ties to China:
_"True the Vote filed a motion seeking the recusal of Judge Kenneth Hoyt in Konnech's litigation against us. That motion was GRANTED today. Recusals in federal court are rare, and this decision reflects the gravity of this matter. Judge Hoyt sought unbridled discovery in this case before the most basic elements were litigated and sent Catherine Engelbrecht and Gregg Phillips to prison without bond, all in a civil matter._
_He was reversed and admonished by the 5th Circuit Court of Appeals._
[](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=ab6c7c6780&e=ad8cba7e98)
_Konnech has sought to deflect attention from their wrongdoing through litigation, and Judge Hoyt's removal from this matter is a big step forward in unwinding the truth of Konnech's role in U.S. elections and elections around the world._
_As has become clear in recent weeks, Chinese incursions into all elements of American society is growing, and our elections are no exception._
_True the Vote looks forward to a full public conversation about the issues of election security raised in this important case.”_
|
---
**Rebuttal | Liberty is not well served by ballot harvesting**
We’ll keep this short for now. Advocating for ballot harvesting isn’t a winning strategy. For that reason, this WSJ article was very difficult to read - [“Donald Trump Changes Tune on Mail-in Voting, Ballot Collection” ](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=c6b29ab898&e=ad8cba7e98)
We’ll talk about this at length on Monday night’s Locals ( 2/20, 7p et). Please join us if you can - [truethevote.locals.com](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=9849772d3f&e=ad8cba7e98)
[](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=72626a3618&e=ad8cba7e98)
|
---
[](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=5ad78c8784&e=ad8cba7e98)
|
---
| | Digging through the True the Vote archives, I found an article I wrote in 2012 titled [“](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=05fb4d9bf4&e=ad8cba7e98) _[Welcome to Your Wake Up Call**”**](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=f686867b93&e=ad8cba7e98)_**.** I can assure you that I didn’t have a crystal ball or a time machine that transported me to 2022 and back. This piece only demonstrates that the election integrity issues we are dealing with today began well before 2020. Fixing these problems has taken on an even greater urgency.

---
[](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=f5c77bd4d9&e=ad8cba7e98)
Excerpts from a time no-so-different as this....**** “Congratulations, you’ve been chosen. Freedom is under attack, our future is uncertain. You have been chosen to be here, in this country, right now, because you are capable of bearing this historic load, of standing up for America and our fundamental beliefs in life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness. It’s in you..it’s why you’re here...it’s why you’re reading this...and it’s why I’m writing it. Like it or lump it, you’ve been chosen.
It was in early 2008 when I had my “you’re chosen” moment. At no time prior to 2008 was being a “political activist operative” ever, ever, on my radar. I was (and still am, by the way) a mother, a founding member of our church, a board member on our children’s school PTO, a city volunteer, and a small business owner.
I believed that government was best left to the politicians; they could be relied upon to work on behalf of the American people using the Constitution as a guide. We would pay our taxes, and me and mine would be left alone. But somewhere in the political harum-scarum leading up to the November elections, things changed. I watched as our government made decisions that stood in stark contrast to what I believed were nonnegotiable American ideals. For the first time in my life, I scared of what government had become and I saw myself as an unwitting accomplice. So, I looked at my children, my aging parents, my business, my church, my country, and decided I would do something to help protect them, even if I didn’t know what that something was....
Our little group decided to start small, so we looked for a simple project. There was a need for people to work at the polls. There weren’t enough volunteers. And so we thought, this is perfect. It will be a one-day gig. But what happened that day at the polls was much more than we’d ever bargained for. We saw chronic confusion over proper polling place procedures. We saw blatant disregard for inconvenient details in the election code, but most troubling of all, we saw outright, undeniable election fraud.
We watched as people were allowed to vote without showing any identification; they were just passed down the line of election workers and over to the voting booth without question. Or voters who came in with multiple registration cards and when told that one registration number was shown as having voted during early election period, was quick to pull out a second registration card, to which the election worker would respond “Oh, ok, that one works.”
But the most common sightings were those voters who would plainly state “I don’t remember who I’m supposed to vote for.” The election worker would hop up and say, “I can help you with that”, then off to the voting booth they would go, whereupon the election worker would proceed to instruct the voter who to vote for.
So was born True the Vote and before you can say “free and fair,” what was supposed to be a simple exercise in civic duty, became a full-time avocation.
A few amazing things happened on the way to upholding election integrity in our hometown...
**Amazing Thing #1** : We learned that not everyone was thrilled with the work we were doing. We’d honestly believed that True the Vote would transcend politics and be seen as an effort of value to all voters. In fact, we’d stumbled into one of the most controversial, emotionally charged subjects there is. Citizens, working within the system, upholding the law, was apparently more than some could bear.
I was sued, threatened, and harassed. Pictures of my home and details about the finances of our family business were posted online. The leader of the New Black Panther Party made public, televised, statements about his intent to have poll workers affiliated with True the Vote thrown out of the polls. All because we’d dared to get involved.
**Amazing Thing #2** : Even though our work only revealed the tip of the iceberg, people still seemed to grasp the idea that there was an iceberg. Masses of willing volunteers began signing up to help True the Vote. It was as if a dirty little secret had finally been said loudly enough for everyone to hear - election fraud exists, and something must be done about it. People responded, not by passing the buck, but by rolling up their sleeves and helping research the voter rolls and work at the polls.
**Amazing Thing #3** : Turns out, there were people across the country who’d seen what we’d seen, had many of the same concerns about their own elections, and wanted to work with us to bring True the Vote to their state. Overnight we became a leader in a new national movement towards election integrity.
If you’ve read this far you know that protecting the integrity of our elections is something I believe in passionately. I hope you will be moved to get involved, to volunteer in your community to make your elections a shining example of democracy in action. Our country needs millions, literally millions, more poll workers. Talk with anyone who works in elections, and they will be quick to tell you, good volunteers are hard to come by. And when you’re there, working at the polls, you will be engaged in one of the most meaningful ways you can possibly be involved in your government.
What future do we want for our country? Who among the candidates can be entrusted to carry your convictions and make the kinds of decisions you would make for your family? Our vote is where the rubber meets the road.
But somewhere along the way we started taking a hands-off approach to our elections, becoming spectators in a process that works best only when citizens stay actively engaged. Now we are faced with inaccurate voter registries, polling places without volunteers, and little political will to prosecute perpetrators of election fraud. That is reality. And there is no resolution to the problem without citizens showing back up to work at the polls. If not us, then who?
Friends, we stand at a precipice. We can participate in returning our country to its roots of liberty or we can stand silently and watch as tyranny takes hold. Those are the only two choices we have.
But there is still the choice!
These problems can be resolved. And, if we can shore up our elections, the most fundamental process of government, then there is nothing we can’t accomplish together. Make no mistake, what I’m doing, what I’m asking you to do, is not convenient, it’s not easy, it’s not always fun. It would be so much easier if I hadn’t gotten the wake-up call. But I did, and I think you did, too.”.....
Definitely a back to the future moment. [You can read the full piece here.](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=b7a2d24d22&e=ad8cba7e98)****
Better days are ahead, but we must be willing to answer the call, toughen up, and fire walk for a while. We can do this. Stay strong, friends. [**Hope you’ll join us Monday night at 7p et. **](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=cb7510aefb&e=ad8cba7e98)
[](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=0e2d364b1d&e=ad8cba7e98)
| | |
---

True the VotePO Box 3109# 19128Houston, TX 77253-3109

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/profile?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=10c8087c36&e=ad8cba7e98&c=e2841f494a) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=10c8087c36&e=ad8cba7e98&c=e2841f494a).
|
---

name: Jim Jordan
subject: Non-Americans voting in our election
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jim Jordan for Congress
body:
show the Left that I’m not the only American who thinks only Americans should be allowed to vote in our elections.
[Unsubscribe](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_s=bdc33dba83c94541ac91861f91744c75&_t=T26k2zeA-YQtdewoiqRj5MNXPFFmy8_u2o6yN8HC1dwrVJlsgZIM4_D-Xn4qpDrTHW1caqhCpviOMgEIoLRg5THB_LQBbib3iwTmaKXKHPQ%3D) | [Mark as Junk](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_s=bdc33dba83c94541ac91861f91744c75&_t=T26k2zeA-YQtdewoiqRj5MNXPFFmy8_u2o6yN8HC1dwrVJlsgZIM4_D-Xn4qpDrTHW1caqhCpviOMgEIoLRg5THB_LQBbib3iwTmaKXKHPQ%3D)

[](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=bdc33dba83c94541ac91861f91744c75&_e=Uqv7Fw4j1BmUn12_n2lWWEEHT1RwvQugnFRsPSrVGVtgomSf1Kykk_o_QCrqiGPB8fHNzFqn10ieXSsFmeo86yNP8GHPTUOlQCLqV8BKPwbfI0zECv9Opa3gaUh6ahYvmXiIBBAjelJx6P88c7_pB2zBDUmZ9lw2melmRXwRg-HxUtil3cNHWZOKT8SrGv546o2eTHs_bFuJ7qHEQ7LtwwJO2u-Px7Q6BTvehV4jea2pLgylNst3ssk26s56KLHVIQL-TrAgm2jFRnUrZnMPh6MYD5OY1jXDB_LaZAvRVywrfUiuBmYNeiQs7CrlxVkkjagQzQR2HH0g5UZa70fwNqRn3cYR8h13X5QjJHv9YMe98c2ITI1q0fSCaFEt6mC65dgjiVAChDIyqtfJipvHaVP3kNCE8gPJaRviT4xt6ddEW2ohgqe2dvPyt3xOXqSe0RuD-ixh26xQMGa1zaGrpA%3D%3D)
|
[**Petition: Do you agree that illegal immigrants should not be allowed to vote in our elections -> **](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=bdc33dba83c94541ac91861f91744c75&_e=Uqv7Fw4j1BmUn12_n2lWWEEHT1RwvQugnFRsPSrVGVtgomSf1Kykk_o_QCrqiGPB8fHNzFqn10ieXSsFmeo86yNP8GHPTUOlQCLqV8BKPwbfI0zECv9Opa3gaUh6ahYvmXiIBBAjelJx6P88c7_pB2zBDUmZ9lw2melmRXwRg-HxUtil3cNHWZOKT8SrGv546o2eTHs_bFuJ7qHEQ7LtwwJO2u-Px7Q6BTvehV4jea2pLgylNst3ssk26s56KLHVIQL-TrAgm2jFRnUrZnMPh6MYD5OY1jXDB_LaZAvRVywrfUiuBmYNeiQs7CrlxVkkMzYFif7UprcBLEt6uoGpCU5aaPFDlql8qhmw4NLgfIlwHXxykR01kQROB_napip_GdZI7sjeIKoezARveVq_XJiM9nr4UD7ZlGPOSC5yyVWKLTBDtHK4P4ywq3bqdiXozYgXrpRWDsjZPZE8gxHVXg%3D%3D)
Fellow Conservative,

I wanted to share with you another reason why it’s so important that we remain ever vigilant and ALWAYS stay in the fight to defend our great country from dangerous and destructive policies.

Just a few days ago, Republicans in the House of Representatives passed a measure making sure that non-citizens are not allowed vote in Washington DC elections.

Sounds like a no-brainer, right? I mean, who the heck would support a crazy idea like non-citizens voting in American elections?

I’ll tell you who would support it: the vast majority of House Democrats would! **And they did! By a 4-to-1 margin!**

Despite their incessant anti-Trump tropes being laden with claims of “foreign influence in elections”, they call it “controversial” for conservatives to say that only American citizens should vote in American elections. I’m not making this up!

We all know that liberals want to fundamentally change our great country, and this latest move fits their playbook to a tee. They want weaker border security, they don’t support law enforcement, and now they have shown they want to allow non-citizens to vote in our elections.

They weren’t successful this time, but when considering that Biden’s border policy is allowing record levels of illegal immigration, and Democrats are raising record levels of money for their campaigns, we should all be scared for the future of our country if the left makes gains in the next election.

I know you receive a lot of fundraising emails, but if you’ve read this far, I know you share my concern. **[If that’s true, I’m asking you to step up and take action to let me know you join with other patriots like yourself to support the fundamental principles that make America great!](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=bdc33dba83c94541ac91861f91744c75&_e=Uqv7Fw4j1BmUn12_n2lWWEEHT1RwvQugnFRsPSrVGVtgomSf1Kykk_o_QCrqiGPB8fHNzFqn10ieXSsFmeo86yNP8GHPTUOlQCLqV8BKPwbfI0zECv9Opa3gaUh6ahYvmXiIBBAjelJx6P88c7_pB2zBDUmZ9lw2melmRXwRg-HxUtil3cNHWZOKT8SrGv546o2eTHs_bFuJ7qHEQ7LtwwJO2u-Px7Q6BTvehV4jea2pLgylNst3ssk26s56KLHVIQL-TrAgm2jFRnUrZnMPh6MYD5OY1jXDB_LaZAvRVywrfUiuBmYNeiQs7CrlxVkkVX1s0aGc3TgwnZpn1hP15x8hVQIekgvezQToiby_I2GzOkHrBjSvBLQtE9mLMJ5j7P7Tjk7CyrwZZQIQ5tpsUP2kBOK9Jw6lfWSFMnJtdlV2Hc48xGSA62g4eGGvgwxxpMHlGuLDvD1D-PrdDdm73g%3D%3D)**

name: Adrastos, Campaign Manager for Team Cori
subject: FWD: These emails fund a large percentage of our budget to re-elect Cori
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori Bush for Congress
body:
If we're going to re-elect Cori, we can't slow down.
#
Hey, it’s Adrastos.
Last week, I sent you an honest email about the state of our fundraising. In case you missed it, I shared it down below.
**The short of it is that our fundraising is behind and we need your support today.[If you can afford to, please consider chipping in your first $5 donation now.](https://act.coribush.org/go/238?c0=cori-em-20230218.lOGz00&amount=5&refcode=em20230218-2199&t=1&akid=2199%2E1082990%2EIyet_D)**
**[Chip in $5](https://act.coribush.org/go/238?c0=cori-em-20230218.lOGz00&amount=5&refcode=em20230218-2199&t=2&akid=2199%2E1082990%2EIyet_D)**
Thank you for being a part of our movement.
—Adrastos
_\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Adrastos, Campaign Manager
Date: Sat, Feb 11, 2023
Subject: These emails fund a large percentage of our budget to re-elect Cori
To: Peter [dpwillis67@gmail.com]_
Hey — can I be honest with you?
I know that emails like this one might add up in your inbox, I see it in mine too! I understand that the stream of emails asking for a donation can seem endless, so I wanted to make sure you knew that a real person wrote this email.
**As Cori's Campaign Manager, I'm reaching out today because the truth is, emails like this one fund a large percentage of our budget to re-elect Cori.**
Last month, we didn't hit our fundraising goal. And now, we're having to work extra hard to make up that gap. _That's where you come in, Peter._
Our opponents are already raising millions from corporate PACs and lobbyists to defeat our movement in 2024, so we can't afford to start this year off financially behind. **But according to our records, it looks like you have yet to chip in to power our campaign to re-elect Cori.**
We understand you may not have been in a position to donate before. But with so much at stake in these next two years, we're hoping we can count on your support today.
[**Will you consider making your first donation to help re-elect Cori today? A donation now will help us get this year back on track to ensure we can put all our energy into fighting for the people.**](https://act.coribush.org/go/238?c0=cori-em-20230218.lOGz00&amount=5&refcode=em20230218-2199&t=5&akid=2199%2E1082990%2EIyet_D)
**[Chip in $5](https://act.coribush.org/go/238?c0=cori-em-20230218.lOGz00&amount=5&refcode=em20230218-2199&t=6&akid=2199%2E1082990%2EIyet_D)**
To many, 2023 is an 'off-year'. But Cori has made it abundantly clear that for us — working people and families — we can't afford to take an off-year.
Not while corporate PACs and lobbyists are already fundraising to take us down next year.
Not while reproductive freedom, Medicare for All, and housing justice are on the line.
And not while right-wing extremists have their eyes on our campaign to see if we have what it takes to re-elect Missouri's first Black Congresswoman.
**[If we're going to re-elect Cori, we can't slow down. Please make your first donation today to support Cori as she presses forward on our progressive agenda for racial, economic, and environmental justice.](https://act.coribush.org/go/238?c0=cori-em-20230218.lOGz00&refcode=em20230218-2199&t=7&akid=2199%2E1082990%2EIyet_D)**
Thank you,
Adrastos
Campaign Manager, Team Cori
_*This data only reflects donations made online with this email address. If you've made a previous contribution via check or with a different email address, you may not see it here — but know that Cori and our entire team are deeply grateful for your support._

**[Contribute ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cori-em-20230218?refcode=em20230218-2199-footer&t=8&refcode2=2199_1082990_Iyet_D&akid=2199%2E1082990%2EIyet_D) **
---
Follow us on **[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/VoteCoriBush/)** , **[Twitter](https://twitter.com/CoriBush)** , **[Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/coribush/)** , and **[visit our website](https://coribush.org/)**. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Mail us: Cori Bush for Congress, 75 North Oaks Plaza, St. Louis, MO 63121.
Paid for by Cori Bush for Congress
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.coribush.org/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.coribush.org/signup/update-your-information?t=9&akid=2199%2E1082990%2EIyet_D)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.coribush.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=10&akid=2199%2E1082990%2EIyet_D)

name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: Lauren Boebert only hung onto her seat by 546 votes, during a midterm election. There's no reason to think Adam Frisch won't beat Boebert this time if he has the resources he needs, in what will be a presidential year with higher Democratic turnout in Colorado. Can you help Frisch report a big launch week?
body:
Walking back the crazy
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0c09ccf8-da2e-4d2c-a41b-98246f071415/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
|
---
| |
---
| | | | | Peter, **_This is so good!!_** _**Adam Frisch, who only lost to the loathsome Lauren Boebert by 546 votes, just announced this week that he’s running against her again in 2024!!**_ **_There's no reason to think Frisch won't beat Boebert if he has the resources he needs, in what will be a presidential year with higher Democratic turnout _****_in Colorado_**** _._** [**_Can you RUSH Adam Frisch $5, $10 or $25 or whatever you can for a HUGE launch week and a great start, making up 547 votes and ridding our Capitol of seditionist Lauren Boebert?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5e4240fb-6ca3-4ac2-a4ef-914ef86c0af9/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) From his announcement on Twitter: [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T46a4fd80-f99e-4c30-903f-2a7736010170/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _**Meanwhile, Boebert either didn't learn a thing from her close shave or is simply unable to learn. August of 2022 her neighbor called 911 because Boebert and her husband--who was arrested in the past for exposing himself to teenage girls at a bowling alley--threatened them.**_ That's a sentence I never imagined having to write.
---
|
---
| | | | | | [**DONATE NOW**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T580c6b89-6b25-4fc3-bb82-ae8f190a732b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| **_Boebert wants to arrest everyone who investigated Trump, impeach President Biden--just because--and defund Ukraine so Putin can annihilate the country. These are the positions of a fascist. Just because she's stupid, don't let it convince you she's not dangerous. She's both._** [**_Can you RUSH Adam Frisch $5, $10 or $25 or whatever you can for a HUGE launch and great start, making up 547 votes and ridding our Capitol of seditionist Lauren Boebert?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T149b35e6-78bc-45a8-8443-a1a928f19a4b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) **This race is so important. We greatly thank you for contributing.** Cliff
---
| | |

|
---
| | | | [**DONATE NOW**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T592ab59f-7537-4f2d-8aa3-f046e7fd069e/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

|

1776 Mentor Ave. #411 | Norwood, Ohio 45212
513.438.0171 | info@blueampaction.com

| Having trouble viewing this email? [View it in your web browser](https://blueampstrategies.salsalabs.org/frischannounceschallengetoboebert4-n?wvpId=368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | |
---
| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T99ee7b6a-d7f0-483d-9873-631a27663855/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---

**But we need to mobilize every Fourth District voter in this critical special election to get it done.** _That’s why we’re expanding our essential outreach programs across the district!_

Peter, one of the most proven-effective ways to spread Jenn’s message is by knocking doors, organizing communities, and distributing campaign materials all over VA-04:

[_**If everyone reading this email chipped in $5, $10, or more we’d have the resources to reach thousands of voters. Peter, we’re so proud of the work Jenn has accomplished in Virginia.

**Having dedicated nearly two decades of public service to helping people and solving problems, Jenn is the _only_ leader for the job.**

We need Jenn fighting for all of us, Peter — because Virginians deserve a results-driven leader in Congress.

_But, Peter, it’ll take all of us, chipping in when we can, to propel Jenn to Washington!_

[_**So with only days to go, it’s never been more important to reach voters in every corner of VA-04 and make sure they know how, when, and where they can vote. The right-wing attacks on reproductive rights did not end with the Supreme Court’s overturning of _Roe v. Wade_. As early as next week, a Donald Trump-nominated federal judge could rule to remove mifepristone, an FDA-approved abortion pill, from the market nationwide. This includes states like New Mexico with strong reproductive rights laws. The court case has huge implications for reproductive healthcare access in the United States — **more than half of women who end their pregnancies use medication abortion.** Martin is fighting to pass legislation in the Senate to protect a woman’s right to make her own healthcare decisions. Do you agree with the majority of Americans who believe in the right to choose? NEGOP Legislative Update Week Ending February 18, 2023
The Unicameral had another busy week with critical hearings. High-profile bills receiving hearings this week were efforts to limit the control of unelected health directors, preserving women’s sports for biological women, and evolving the ESG conversation. As we mentioned last week, we would begin to see bills passed out of committee and into the general file (on the path towards a floor vote). The party has been watching crucial bills that advance the key planks of our legislative plan or promote our platform. We saw four economic bills, two public safety bills, one government reform, and one Constitutional rights bill advance to the general file. We are happy with the progress of bills moving to the next stage. This was the first major week of this stage in the legislative process. More testifying and effort is needed in the coming weeks to keep the momentum going. Of the bills we are watching, a Constitutional amendment (that is receiving very little coverage by the media) advanced to the General File was Senator Briese’s LR14CA. This Constitutional amendment would be placed on the ballot in 2024 and would enable a recall of the Governor, Lieutenant Governor, Secretary of State, Auditor of Public Accounts, Attorney General, or a State Legislator. A recall provision would be a very powerful tool enacted to remove an elected official at any time. We believe this is important to keep government leaders in check by the people. Next week, additional committee hearings will be had on important bills. Legislation to limit what political subdivisions (local taxing bodies) can do on property tax, remedies for veterans that suffered setbacks due to Covid vaccines, and public safety issues around protecting children in law in ALL situations of abuse from ALL offenders. Senator Albrecht has two separate bills up for hearings. The first is a bill to adopt the Nebraska Pregnancy Health Act. The NEGOP is supportive of promoting proactive measures to help women in tough pregnancy situations. The second bill aims to protect the ability of citizens to speak at meetings subject to the Open Meetings Act. Protecting the Constitutional rights of our citizens remains a primary goal and we understand the battles of free speech that are being limited or violated at many ‘Open Meetings Act’ meetings...especially school boards. We encourage you to keep coming to Lincoln to make your voice heard! The rest of the update... ESG (Environmental, social, and governance (ESG) investing is used to screen investments based on corporate policies) Introductory bills were heard on lending rights and the investments of the state with companies that have ESG policies that work against the economic interests of Nebraska. Financial lobbies and environmentalists testified against these measures. Cattle ranchers, gas station owners, firearms dealers, ag interests, and a University finance professor testified in favor of these bills. A member of the Nebraska Investment Council provided an informative neutral position on the bill to advance the debate. Much of the debate centered around a consensus that no issues have arisen yet in the local banks. However, proponents testified a ‘preemptive strike’ is needed for what looks like a problem on the horizon. It is clear the state will be moving forward with this issue but it is hard to say if the two bills debated this week will make it out of committee. The conscious thought is that our important financial institutions in this state will need to come to the table with all economic players to craft smart solutions to address ESG which has many of our industries concerned across the state. Banks and industries are important to this state and we need these entities working together to advance our economy. We remain committed to a preemptive strike versus a reactionary policy on this issue. Proactive, not reactive, is a new theme in this party we will continue to advocate for. Pro-LIfe No word on the heartbeat bill coming out of committee....yet. However, as mentioned in the opening paragraph, the Nebraska Pregnancy Health Act is key partnering legislation with the heartbeat bill that needs to advance will be heard in committee this week. Protecting Women’s Rights Senator Kauth introduced her bill to protect women’s sports and bathrooms. The bill would prohibit biological men from participating in women’s sports and occupying women’s bathrooms. Democrats testified in force. Individuals were bused in from around the country with outcries of discrimination and rights violations. The NEGOP remains committed to this goal to ensure scholarship opportunities remain for women, a level playing field is created for biological sexes, and bathrooms remain protected space for each sex. Stopping Tyranny An interesting and important bill was debated in committee that would reduce the power of local unelected health directors. Cities like Omaha and Lincoln were severely affected by the mandates of unelected health directors during the Covid pandemic. Church services being silenced, business doors being closed, and many other negative outcomes were experienced with unilateral action done by health directors with no oversight by legislative bodies. Republican Matt Schulte, a County Commissioner in Lancaster County, is seeking to end a state of emergency that still exists in the Capital City! There is no justification of a state of emergency to exist in perpetuity when the rest of the state and country have moved on. This state of emergency is strongly related to overreaching Health Directors who are not accountable to the voting public. Limiting the power of unelected bureaucrats is necessary to protect religious freedom, our economy, and Constitutional rights of Nebraskans. Energy Senator Bostelman continues to drive forward issues surrounding energy reliability and affordability. We think his work is very important to our future. Yet this work receives little media attention because it lacks “Hot Topic” headline material. Senator Bostelman has already had hearings on bills that; move elections of officers for utilities to a partisan ballot, prohibits financial conflicts of interest at utilities, and studies the realities of renewable energy. This week they held hearings to address the workforce training needed in hydrogen and nuclear industries. The party is committed to the plank of affordable and reliable energy. We appreciate the work of Senator Bostelman in this important area. Election Integrity We have received numerous calls on a concern of committee hearings regarding election bills being canceled. The reality is that fiscal footnotes were not received in a timely manner and input from the Attorney General office was needed. We anticipate these hearings will still occur and were just delayed for these issues. We have heard the rumors of a special session on this topic. The election integrity committee is not supportive of a special session and we feel this topic needs to be dealt with during this session. The legislation around the Constitutional amendment for Voter ID must be passed to conduct future credible statewide elections. Stay tuned.. Public Safety The state troopers and the Governor signed a new deal that significantly increases the pay for our state troopers across the state. Ensuring Public Safety is a defining Platform Plank of the NEGOP and this was a needed step in the right direction. In an era where public safety officers remain in high demand across the country, we have to be supportive and proactive to maintain staffing and recruitment levels for Nebraska's law enforcement departments. Taking care of those who take care of us makes sense. We are glad this deal got done! Dealing With an Inept Washington The Governor signed on to a letter expressing concern with the President’s lack of response to the invasion of our air space by the Chinese. The quiet whisper is that senior military officials are very concerned about this invasion not being detected. Furthermore, the slow response to this invasion of our air space is incredibly troublesome. National security is a top Constitutional priority of our federal government and they are failing miserably at the border and now detecting violations of our air space. We need change in DC. We need DC to focus on their Constitutional obligations and not on power unenumerated in the Constitution. On a separate issue, Attorney General Hilgers signed on with other Attorney Generals from around the country to stop the Democrats endless obsession with the WOTUS rules (Waters of the United States). These rules severely affect Nebraska Agriculture. “Navigable Water” was redefined to include ditches, ponds, etc by the federal government who cannot respond appropriately to train derailments but feel called to manage ditches to save the world. I’m not sure if they boat down the ditches in DC (this would not surprise us), but they do not boat down the ditches in Nebraska. We appreciate the continued push back against these intrusive rules that lack common sense and hurt Nebraska agriculture. Citizen Engagement - Apathy can not Win Continued discussion is being had to determine if citizens are ready to become more involved in their Republic or if they are looking for other people to handle their responsibilities. So many new people have stepped up to the plate, but many citizens remain on the sidelines. Specialization is not a needed component in a Republic. All citizens must engage. Our county leaders from around the state are preparing the county parties to help turn the tide of progressivism that has swept our state and has gone unchecked. Democrats have grown from 400 to 900 local elected offices the past decade. This has radically redefined taxes, schools, elections, and more. The question that remains...Will the citizens engage or acquiesce? We encourage you and your neighbors to engage the work, the donations, and support. Our county leaders have the tools to put you to work to make a lasting change. Help us help you. Become a monthly donor to your state AND county party. Become a volunteer. Support your Republican Candidates. Until Next Week.... Todd WatsonNEGOP Political DirectorPolitical@ne.gop

clean_body: NEGOP Legislative Update Week Ending February 18, 2023 The Unicameral had another busy week with critical hearings. High-profile bills recei | | Can't see this message? | | | | | | | 108th Legislative Session"2ND HOUSE ALERT/WEEKLY UPDATE"February 18, 2023 | | | | | | | Get Our Second House Alerts | | | | | | | NEGOP Legislative Update Week Ending February 18, 2023 The Unicameral had another busy week with critical hearings. High-profile bills receiving hearings this week were efforts to limit the control of unelected health directors, preserving women’s sports for biological women, and evolving the ESG conversation. As we mentioned last week, we would begin to see bills passed out of committee and into the general file (on the path towards a floor vote). The party has been watching crucial bills that advance the key planks of our legislative plan or promote our platform. We saw four economic bills, two public safety bills, one government reform, and one Constitutional rights bill advance to the general file. We are happy with the progress of bills moving to the next stage. This was the first major week of this stage in the legislative process. More testifying and effort is needed in the coming weeks to keep the momentum going. Of the bills we are watching, a Constitutional amendment (that is receiving very little coverage by the media) advanced to the General File was Senator Briese’s LR14CA. This Constitutional amendment would be placed on the ballot in 2024 and would enable a recall of the Governor, Lieutenant Governor, Secretary of State, Auditor of Public Accounts, Attorney General, or a State Legislator. A recall provision would be a very powerful tool enacted to remove an elected official at any time. We believe this is important to keep government leaders in check by the people. Next week, additional committee hearings will be had on important bills. Legislation to limit what political subdivisions (local taxing bodies) can do on property tax, remedies for veterans that suffered setbacks due to Covid vaccines, and public safety issues around protecting children in law in ALL situations of abuse from ALL offenders. Senator Albrecht has two separate bills up for hearings. The first is a bill to adopt the Nebraska Pregnancy Health Act. The NEGOP is supportive of promoting proactive measures to help women in tough pregnancy situations. The second bill aims to protect the ability of citizens to speak at meetings subject to the Open Meetings Act. Protecting the Constitutional rights of our citizens remains a primary goal and we understand the battles of free speech that are being limited or violated at many ‘Open Meetings Act’ meetings...especially school boards. We encourage you to keep coming to Lincoln to make your voice heard! The rest of the update... ESG (Environmental, social, and governance (ESG) investing is used to screen investments based on corporate policies) Introductory bills were heard on lending rights and the investments of the state with companies that have ESG policies that work against the economic interests of Nebraska. Financial lobbies and environmentalists testified against these measures. Cattle ranchers, gas station owners, firearms dealers, ag interests, and a University finance professor testified in favor of these bills. A member of the Nebraska Investment Council provided an informative neutral position on the bill to advance the debate. Much of the debate centered around a consensus that no issues have arisen yet in the local banks. However, proponents testified a ‘preemptive strike’ is needed for what looks like a problem on the horizon. It is clear the state will be moving forward with this issue but it is hard to say if the two bills debated this week will make it out of committee. The conscious thought is that our important financial institutions in this state will need to come to the table with all economic players to craft smart solutions to address ESG which has many of our industries concerned across the state. Banks and industries are important to this state and we need these entities working together to advance our economy. We remain committed to a preemptive strike versus a reactionary policy on this issue. Proactive, not reactive, is a new theme in this party we will continue to advocate for. Pro-LIfe No word on the heartbeat bill coming out of committee....yet. However, as mentioned in the opening paragraph, the Nebraska Pregnancy Health Act is key partnering legislation with the heartbeat bill that needs to advance will be heard in committee this week. Protecting Women’s Rights Senator Kauth introduced her bill to protect women’s sports and bathrooms. The bill would prohibit biological men from participating in women’s sports and occupying women’s bathrooms. Democrats testified in force. Individuals were bused in from around the country with outcries of discrimination and rights violations. The NEGOP remains committed to this goal to ensure scholarship opportunities remain for women, a level playing field is created for biological sexes, and bathrooms remain protected space for each sex. Stopping Tyranny An interesting and important bill was debated in committee that would reduce the power of local unelected health directors. Cities like Omaha and Lincoln were severely affected by the mandates of unelected health directors during the Covid pandemic. Church services being silenced, business doors being closed, and many other negative outcomes were experienced with unilateral action done by health directors with no oversight by legislative bodies. Republican Matt Schulte, a County Commissioner in Lancaster County, is seeking to end a state of emergency that still exists in the Capital City! There is no justification of a state of emergency to exist in perpetuity when the rest of the state and country have moved on. This state of emergency is strongly related to overreaching Health Directors who are not accountable to the voting public. Limiting the power of unelected bureaucrats is necessary to protect religious freedom, our economy, and Constitutional rights of Nebraskans. Energy Senator Bostelman continues to drive forward issues surrounding energy reliability and affordability. We think his work is very important to our future. Yet this work receives little media attention because it lacks “Hot Topic” headline material. Senator Bostelman has already had hearings on bills that; move elections of officers for utilities to a partisan ballot, prohibits financial conflicts of interest at utilities, and studies the realities of renewable energy. This week they held hearings to address the workforce training needed in hydrogen and nuclear industries. The party is committed to the plank of affordable and reliable energy. We appreciate the work of Senator Bostelman in this important area. Election Integrity We have received numerous calls on a concern of committee hearings regarding election bills being canceled. The reality is that fiscal footnotes were not received in a timely manner and input from the Attorney General office was needed. We anticipate these hearings will still occur and were just delayed for these issues. We have heard the rumors of a special session on this topic. The election integrity committee is not supportive of a special session and we feel this topic needs to be dealt with during this session. The legislation around the Constitutional amendment for Voter ID must be passed to conduct future credible statewide elections. Stay tuned.. Public Safety The state troopers and the Governor signed a new deal that significantly increases the pay for our state troopers across the state. Ensuring Public Safety is a defining Platform Plank of the NEGOP and this was a needed step in the right direction. In an era where public safety officers remain in high demand across the country, we have to be supportive and proactive to maintain staffing and recruitment levels for Nebraska's law enforcement departments. Taking care of those who take care of us makes sense. We are glad this deal got done! Dealing With an Inept Washington The Governor signed on to a letter expressing concern with the President’s lack of response to the invasion of our air space by the Chinese. The quiet whisper is that senior military officials are very concerned about this invasion not being detected. Furthermore, the slow response to this invasion of our air space is incredibly troublesome. National security is a top Constitutional priority of our federal government and they are failing miserably at the border and now detecting violations of our air space. We need change in DC. We need DC to focus on their Constitutional obligations and not on power unenumerated in the Constitution. On a separate issue, Attorney General Hilgers signed on with other Attorney Generals from around the country to stop the Democrats endless obsession with the WOTUS rules (Waters of the United States). These rules severely affect Nebraska Agriculture. “Navigable Water” was redefined to include ditches, ponds, etc by the federal government who cannot respond appropriately to train derailments but feel called to manage ditches to save the world. I’m not sure if they boat down the ditches in DC (this would not surprise us), but they do not boat down the ditches in Nebraska. We appreciate the continued push back against these intrusive rules that lack common sense and hurt Nebraska agriculture. Citizen Engagement - Apathy can not Win Continued discussion is being had to determine if citizens are ready to become more involved in their Republic or if they are looking for other people to handle their responsibilities. So many new people have stepped up to the plate, but many citizens remain on the sidelines. Specialization is not a needed component in a Republic. All citizens must engage. Our county leaders from around the state are preparing the county parties to help turn the tide of progressivism that has swept our state and has gone unchecked. Democrats have grown from 400 to 900 local elected offices the past decade. This has radically redefined taxes, schools, elections, and more. The question that remains...Will the citizens engage or acquiesce? We encourage you and your neighbors to engage the work, the donations, and support. Our county leaders have the tools to put you to work to make a lasting change. Help us help you. Become a monthly donor to your state AND county party. Become a volunteer. Support your Republican Candidates. Until Next Week.... Todd WatsonNEGOP Political DirectorPolitical@ne.gop Tools for the Legislative Action Below: Video Tutorial: William Feely, Committeeman and Legislative Director Written Tutorial: Sue Greenwald, clean_body: | | | For the sake of our state and our country, we’ve got to take back the House of Representatives so we can remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from her positions on some of the most powerful Congressional committees. Here’s why: She humiliated herself and our state by booing and jeering at President Biden during the State of the Union. She’s an extremist who spouts antisemitic and racist conspiracy theories. She’s spreading dangerous conspiracy theories about vaccinations and COVID-19. She denied the legitimacy of the 2020 election, putting our democracy at risk! Now that the GOP has the House majority, Marjorie Taylor Greene is serving on some of the most powerful committees in Congress and has the ear of the Speaker of the House. Peter, we can’t let this stand! = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = = = = If we band together, we can stop Marjorie Taylor Greene. –GA Dems | | Paid for by the Democratic Party of Georgia. Fellow Conservative,

I wanted to share with you another reason why it’s so important that we remain ever vigilant and ALWAYS stay in the fight to defend our great country from dangerous and destructive policies.

Just a few days ago, Republicans in the House of Representatives passed a measure making sure that non-citizens are not allowed vote in Washington DC elections.

Sounds like a no-brainer, right? I mean, who the heck would support a crazy idea like non-citizens voting in American elections?

I’ll tell you who would support it: the vast majority of House Democrats would! **And they did! By a 4-to-1 margin!**

Despite their incessant anti-Trump tropes being laden with claims of “foreign influence in elections”, they call it “controversial” for conservatives to say that only American citizens should vote in American elections. I’m not making this up!

We all know that liberals want to fundamentally change our great country, and this latest move fits their playbook to a tee. They want weaker border security, they don’t support law enforcement, and now they have shown they want to allow non-citizens to vote in our elections.

They weren’t successful this time, but when considering that Biden’s border policy is allowing record levels of illegal immigration, and Democrats are raising record levels of money for their campaigns, we should all be scared for the future of our country if the left makes gains in the next election.

I know you receive a lot of fundraising emails, but if you’ve read this far, I know you share my concern. **[If that’s true, I’m asking you to step up and take action to let me know you join with other patriots like yourself to support the fundamental principles that make America great!](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=cb20740f4efb4f79966829498ca1cde4&_e=rMpIepuBdPknTsbhsIPRfCtBTgHTNGWDB2tUwb8jdAEAqz6lLATRolbYxJCjJfENEXQP8dtsQ-GIHShx_Gg4-DZgiqOpSc23SFBXrVXtiE5KiTX2iQHOYdYKP9sv_XlwzKlaQ3tIz-cZpS6W5Uuy9Spp_E6RhEwz6QBa4SkbRZqk7Alnk34oMplSLZ3k_bR3q13GCDNmMhsZIzifvjGU8_OJo42P_56eCjH97WAzNNoer6NuJ407dCiirxWJkfSeBmCM4tbIH7aX5NHsU_jQ-U_5_PgsUop_D1xaYL19OrGji-54FWER7awCS_VHgSdedkl-S1YSlKHGcKGjTQronZT4555zPf5CCv3UWUREFmus5unMExIE3V6JJ9R_GX-XSqniBuyug6bKTeXj58wfm4WAk2gPNkE9K6PviATiHXR6-QwKXzOyfdqS4pMIey7HSw6gN9K_kdTekTWudwOw5g%3D%3D)**
