**About Jim Jordan:** | Leading the fight to hold Washington accountable.

**
In Washington, Jim Jordan is focused on his oversight role, helping lead investigations into federal agencies

In Congress, Jim Jordan continued to fight for fiscal responsibility,and he offered the only balanced budget of any member of the House or Senate.

As a state legislator, he took on the powerful special interests and the leaders from both political parties who wanted to put Ohio on an unsustainable path of higher taxes and spending.

He built a conservative, pro-family voting record, and authored an alternative state budget that balanced without raising taxes. He became the only legislator in state history to win both the Defender of Life award from Ohio Right to Life and the Pro-Life Legislator of the Year award from the United Conservatives of Ohio.

name: Katie Porter
subject: Reclaiming YOUR time
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
In the interest of reclaiming YOUR time that you spend reading fundraising emails and thinking "get to the point"
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=17947%2E3422442%2EmqunUn)
Derek,
If you’ve ever watched a Congressional Hearing, you know that I’m often saying "_reclaiming my time_ " as witnesses try to avoid answering questions about how they’ve taken advantage of taxpayers, shortchanged employees, or lied to Americans.
So in the interest of reclaiming YOUR time that you spend reading fundraising emails and thinking get to the point, here’s just a few facts about why you should donate, and **[why I’m seemingly pleading for your $5.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230218-1?amount=5&refcode=em20230218-1&refcode2=17947_3422442_mqunUn&akid=17947%2E3422442%2EmqunUn)**
* I’m running to represent California in the U.S. Senate because in my few years in Washington, I’ve seen how those with power are too beholden to special interests. I’ve spent my career standing up to big corporations and fighting corruption, and I’m ready to take our fight to the U.S. Senate.
* I’m the only candidate in this race who has never taken a dime from corporate PACs. I’m one of the few Congresspeople to reject federal lobbyist money and contributions from Big Oil, Big Pharma, and Big Bank CEOs.

Winning in a huge state like California takes serious resources. As a single parent, I know a thing or two about planning ahead—and I’m putting that same energy into our campaign. **[Can you donate $5 today to support our campaign today?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230218-1?amount=5&refcode=em20230218-1&refcode2=17947_3422442_mqunUn&akid=17947%2E3422442%2EmqunUn)**
**[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230218-1?amount=5&refcode=EMAF18_1_YOU_20230218_V1_F&refcode2=17947_3422442_mqunUn&akid=17947%2E3422442%2EmqunUn)**
Your contribution will help us prepare and plan for the competitive race ahead. Growing a large base of grassroots donors like you is one of our first steps to winning this race.
Thanks a ton,
Katie

Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-footer?refcode=EMAF18_1_YOU_20230218_V1_F&t=2&refcode2=17947_3422442_mqunUn&akid=17947%2E3422442%2EmqunUn)**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4s-emails/?t=3&akid=17947%2E3422442%2EmqunUn)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4s-em-update-info-footer)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4s-unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=17947%2E3422442%2EmqunUn)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Senate
PO Box 5176
Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)
Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate

Dear Peter,

As natural disasters like wildfires, hurricanes, floods, and draughts continue to escalate year over year, including Hurricane Ida, which the Washington Post recently called "the poster child for a climate change-driven disaster", the health and safety of our communities is at risk and people are suffering ? especially Black and Indigenous communities, communities of color, and low-wealth communities who are already being disproportionately harmed and displaced.

However bleak the future may seem, I'm here to tell you ? there is hope.

The most important climate legislation in a generation is being debated as you read this, Peter.

Up for consideration in both the U.S. House and Senate is a bill that would put serious resources into clean energy. Funding for clean water. Clean air. Public lands. Environmental justice. There's a shot ? a real shot ? at passage.

Our movement is fighting every day for our Earth, and we are making real progress. The pro-environment majority in Congress and pro-climate leaders in the Biden-Harris administration have a chance to enact impactful legislation for a clean energy economy, investments in environmental justice, and thousands of good-paying, union jobs.

But this fight won't be over in a year, or even 5 years. At LCV, we're planning for the long-haul which is why we're bringing together our entire movement for an informative, inspiring, one-of-a-kind event later this month ? LCV's Climate Action Watch Party.

Want to get access to unique advocacy resources, up-to-the-minute insider information, and hear from the most important environmental leaders of our time? Register today ? https://links.messages.lcv.

https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/YYbZBye-VkSw/TGRkaFJQOGlyd0hoRitEQjQ0elZ5L1EyM1EwdTRUMGR4S24rQTlNNngwTGhrVnpJeUJrRW1CQXE1YW5hUmRrWUwveTlNRk9PbDF0bUlHV3hZS090QUViZDVKSlBEeFR3V2MyL1ZMSTl1U1U9S0/
What is LCV's Climate Action Watch Party you might be asking? On Wednesday, September 22, 2021 https://links.messages.lcv. we are bringing our community of thousands of members together to hear from climate champions from across the movement and get plugged into our plan for climate action.

Ticket sales will go directly to our field campaigns, but also grant you entrance to one of our biggest events of the year, where you can hear from Senator Catherine Cortez Masto, Representative Lisa Blunt Rochester, and more to be announced!

Our movement is fighting every day to protect our planet and everyone who inhabits it, and we are making real progress. There's a real chance to enact impactful legislation for a clean energy economy, investments in environmental justice, and thousands of good-paying, unionized jobs.

If everyone reading this joins, we will be able to ensure our organizers have what they need to reach every voter and community leader where it matters most. Your continued support helps keep organizers knocking on doors and ads airing across channels, and ultimately pass the reconciliation bill. There is not a moment to waste.

Thank you for helping us keep up the pressure ? progress is only possible when we work together.

Onward,
Gene Karpisnki
LCV President
? https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/6Lg6B~GrpNQR/TGRkaFJQOGlyd0hoRitEQjQ0elZ5L1EyM1EwdTRUMGR4S24rQTlNNngwTGhrVnpJeUJrRW1CQXE1YW5hUmRrWUwveTlNRk9PbDF0bUlHV3hZS090QUViZDVKSlBEeFR3V2MyL1ZMSTl1U1U9S0/bVNlRFhCTFhwcFBiTFVkclZFOTYwK2VUS2hWanFwT2ZDc1ltS3o1WGt0Nm00bkhsZlVhdGRPMFo5bTlZQlh4b1lyZFFwM1d0Sk1ZRmQ1bGJmOXdmdnphNzJPRmRzVVpIS0 ? ? ? ? ? ? name: Lucas Kunce
subject: This wasn’t part of my plan.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Upset The Setup PAC
body:
Here’s what they all have in common:

| Hey Peter, it’s Lucas Kunce. I wanted to take some time today to tell you a little bit about where I come from and what got me into Missouri’s U.S. Senate race. **Will you please give me a moment to explain?**

As a kid from a working-class neighborhood in the heart of Central Missouri, running for U.S. Senate was never part of my plan. [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58920926/392666193/-1552608438?amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,250&refcode=UTS_20230218_EMSPL_E2_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5NDc2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjIxYWYxZjI3LWU0YWYtZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=A6qFiN2KShej6TtiJIJsEJwyyK9aZqJiWUhSi-Tti1g=) Like so many Americans, my parents lived paycheck-to-paycheck — so when my little sister was born with a heart condition, the medical bills bankrupted us. Maxed-out credit cards and no money left, times had never been tougher.

**But we weren’t alone — our community took care of us.**

When my parents had to take my sister to the hospital in St. Louis, our neighbors and friends took my siblings and me into their homes. And when we couldn’t afford groceries, they brought more casserole and lasagna by the house than we could ever hope to eat. That’s the Missouri I grew up in.

Their support helped send me to college, and after that I joined the Marines to honor everything they had done for me and my family growing up.

When I came home between tours in Iraq and Afghanistan, one of the hardest things was seeing what was happening back in my old neighborhood. It was a community picked for parts — the local corner store boarded up, my first family house bulldozed to the ground. **Here’s all that’s left of it:** [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/58920927/392666194/-1552608438?amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100,250&refcode=UTS_20230218_EMSPL_E2_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5NDc2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjIxYWYxZjI3LWU0YWYtZWQxMS05OTRkLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=A6qFiN2KShej6TtiJIJsEJwyyK9aZqJiWUhSi-Tti1g=) While all of us were risking our lives building up towns like Habbaniyah, Fallujah, and Herat, career politicians like Josh Hawley let our country waste away. They spent trillions on bailouts, tax breaks, and subsidies for the industries that stripped our communities for parts — like Wall Street, Big Pharma, and Big Oil — and then told us they can’t figure out how to invest even a fraction of that into building up our own country and investing in our own people.

clean_body: Here’s what they all have in common: | Hey Peter, it’s Lucas Kunce. I wanted to take some time today to tell you a little bit about where I come from and what got me into Missouri’s U.S. Senate race. Will you please give me a moment to explain? As a kid from a working-class neighborhood in the heart of Central Missouri, running for U.S. Senate was never part of my plan. = Like so many Americans, my parents lived paycheck-to-paycheck — so when my little sister was born with a heart condition, the medical bills bankrupted us. Maxed-out credit cards and no money left, times had never been tougher. But we weren’t alone — our community took care of us. When my parents had to take my sister to the hospital in St. Louis, our neighbors and friends took my siblings and me into their homes. And when we couldn’t afford groceries, they brought more casserole and lasagna by the house than we could ever hope to eat. That’s the Missouri I grew up in. Their support helped send me to college, and after that I joined the Marines to honor everything they had done for me and my family growing up. When I came home between tours in Iraq and Afghanistan, one of the hardest things was seeing what was happening back in my old neighborhood. It was a community picked for parts — the local corner store boarded up, my first family house bulldozed to the ground. Here’s all that’s left of it: = While all of us were risking our lives building up towns like Habbaniyah, Fallujah, and Herat, career politicians like Josh Hawley let our country waste away. They spent trillions on bailouts, tax breaks, and subsidies for the industries that stripped our communities for parts — like Wall Street, Big Pharma, and Big Oil — and then told us they can’t figure out how to invest even a fraction of that into building up our own country and investing in our own people. I’m running for U.S. Senate because it’s time everyday Missourians — people who know how to take care of each other — called the shots in our country.= = If you’re ready to take back power from the massive corporations and corrupt, career politicians who’ve torn up our country, I need you on this team.= If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Lucas Kunce and Upset the Setup will go through immediately: | = = = = = Thank you for everything, Peter.
